reason: Modifying /etc/fstab mount options for the /boot partition carries a risk of system boot failure if the syntax is incorrect or if the specific filesystem requires certain device access. While 'nodev' is a standard hardening practice, any change to boot-critical mount points should be verified by a human to ensure the system remains bootable.
estimated_impact: reboot required; filesystems affected; Incorrect /etc/fstab syntax can prevent system boot; Changes to /boot mount options affect the ability to load kernels and initramfs
